1	"""
2	Training script for the multi-type IDSP EQ Estimator.
3	
4	Uses StreamingTCNModel with MultiTypeEQLoss (Hungarian matching,
5	permutation-invariant parameter regression, type classification).
6	Implements curriculum learning from conf/config.yaml stages.
7	"""
8	
9	import math
10	
11	import torch
12	from torch.utils.data import DataLoader
13	from model_tcn import StreamingTCNModel
14	from dsp_frontend import STFTFrontend
15	from loss_multitype import MultiTypeEQLoss, HungarianBandMatcher
16	from dataset import SyntheticEQDataset, collate_fn, FILTER_NAMES
17	import yaml
18	from pathlib import Path
19	import time
20	import json
21	import gc
22	import random
23	import numpy as np
24	
25	
26	def load_config(path="conf/config.yaml"):
27	    with open(path, "r") as f:
28	        return yaml.safe_load(f)
29	
30	
31	# Map filter type names to indices used in dataset
32	FILTER_TYPE_TO_IDX = {name: idx for idx, name in enumerate(FILTER_NAMES)}
33	
34	
35	class Trainer:
36	    def __init__(self, config_path="conf/config.yaml", resume_from=None):
37	        self.cfg = load_config(config_path)
38	
39	        # Seed for reproducibility
40	        seed = self.cfg.get("seed", 42)
41	        torch.manual_seed(seed)
42	        torch.cuda.manual_seed_all(seed)
43	        np.random.seed(seed)
44	        random.seed(seed)
45	        torch.backends.cudnn.deterministic = trainer_cfg.get(
46	            "cudnn_deterministic", False
47	        )
48	        torch.backends.cudnn.benchmark = trainer_cfg.get("cudnn_benchmark", True)
49	
50	        self.device = torch.device("cuda" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu")
51	        self.resume_from = resume_from
52	
53	        data_cfg = self.cfg["data"]
54	        model_cfg = self.cfg["model"]
55	        loss_cfg = self.cfg["loss"]
56	        trainer_cfg = self.cfg["trainer"]
57	
58	        self.num_bands = data_cfg["num_bands"]
59	        self.sample_rate = data_cfg["sample_rate"]
60	        self.n_fft = data_cfg.get("n_fft", 2048)
61	        self.log_every = trainer_cfg["log_every_n_steps"]
62	
63	        # Curriculum config
64	        self.curriculum_cfg = self.cfg.get("curriculum", {})
65	        self.stages = self.curriculum_cfg.get("stages", [])
66	
67	        # Compute total epochs from curriculum stages (or fall back to trainer.max_epochs)
68	        if self.stages:
69	            self.max_epochs = sum(s["epochs"] for s in self.stages)
70	        else:
71	            self.max_epochs = trainer_cfg["max_epochs"]
72	
73	        # Model
74	        enc_cfg = model_cfg.get("encoder", {})
75	        num_filter_types = len(
76	            data_cfg.get(
77	                "filter_types",
78	                ["peaking", "lowshelf", "highshelf", "highpass", "lowpass"],
79	            )
80	        )
81	        use_full_spectrum = model_cfg.get("use_full_spectrum", False)
82	        self.use_full_spectrum = use_full_spectrum
83	        self.model = StreamingTCNModel(
84	            n_mels=data_cfg.get("n_mels", 128),
85	            embedding_dim=enc_cfg.get("embedding_dim", 128),
86	            num_bands=self.num_bands,
87	            channels=enc_cfg.get("channels", 128),
88	            num_blocks=enc_cfg.get("num_blocks", 4),
89	            num_stacks=enc_cfg.get("num_stacks", 2),
90	            sample_rate=self.sample_rate,
91	            n_fft=self.n_fft,
92	            num_filter_types=num_filter_types,
93	            kernel_size=enc_cfg.get("kernel_size", 3),
94	            use_full_spectrum=use_full_spectrum,
95	        ).to(self.device)
96	
97	        # STFT frontend
98	        self.frontend = STFTFrontend(
99	            n_fft=self.n_fft,
100	            hop_length=data_cfg.get("hop_length", 256),
101	            win_length=self.n_fft,
102	            mel_bins=data_cfg.get("n_mels", 128),
103	            sample_rate=self.sample_rate,
104	        ).to(self.device)
105	
106	        # Loss (base weights — curriculum stages can override)
107	        self.base_loss_cfg = loss_cfg
108	        self.criterion = MultiTypeEQLoss(
109	            n_fft=self.n_fft,
110	            sample_rate=self.sample_rate,
111	            lambda_param=loss_cfg.get("lambda_param", 1.0),
112	            lambda_type=loss_cfg.get("lambda_type", 2.0),
113	            lambda_spectral=loss_cfg.get("lambda_spectral", 1.0),
114	            lambda_hmag=loss_cfg.get("lambda_hmag", 0.3),
115	            lambda_activity=loss_cfg.get("lambda_activity", 0.1),
116	            lambda_spread=loss_cfg.get("lambda_spread", 0.05),
117	            lambda_coverage=loss_cfg.get("lambda_coverage", 0.0),
118	            lambda_freq_anchor=loss_cfg.get("lambda_freq_anchor", 0.0),
119	            lambda_freq_match=loss_cfg.get("lambda_freq_match", 1.0),
120	            lambda_q_match=loss_cfg.get("lambda_q_match", 0.5),
121	        ).to(self.device)
122	
123	        self.optimizer = torch.optim.AdamW(
124	            self.model.parameters(),
125	            lr=model_cfg["learning_rate"],
126	            weight_decay=model_cfg["weight_decay"],
127	        )
128	
129	        # Hungarian matcher for permutation-invariant validation metrics
130	        self.val_matcher = HungarianBandMatcher(
131	            lambda_freq=loss_cfg.get("lambda_freq_match", 1.0),
132	            lambda_q=loss_cfg.get("lambda_q_match", 0.5),
133	        )
134	
135	        self.data_cfg = data_cfg
136	        self.global_step = 0
137	        self.best_val_loss = float("inf")
138	        self.history = []
139	
140	        # Checkpoint directory based on experiment name
141	        exp_name = self.cfg.get("experiment_name", "default")
142	        self.ckpt_dir = Path("checkpoints") / exp_name
143	        self.ckpt_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
144	
145	        # Data config for stage transitions
146	        self.train_loader = None
147	        self.val_loader = None
148	        self.test_loader = None
149	        self.test_set = None
150	
151	        # Default scheduler (will be replaced by _setup_stage for curriculum training)
152	        self.scheduler = torch.optim.lr_scheduler.CosineAnnealingLR(
153	            self.optimizer, T_max=self.max_epochs, eta_min=1e-6
154	        )
155	
156	        # Mixed precision training — BF16 on Blackwell GPUs (no GradScaler needed)
157	        # BF16 has the same dynamic range as FP32 (8 exponent bits) so it's
158	        # numerically safer for DSP operations than FP16 (5 exponent bits).
159	        self.use_amp = self.device.type == "cuda" and torch.cuda.is_bf16_supported()
160	        self.amp_dtype = torch.bfloat16 if self.use_amp else torch.float32
161	        print(f"  AMP enabled: {self.use_amp} (dtype={self.amp_dtype})")
162	
163	        # Gradient accumulation: simulates larger effective batch size
164	        # without increasing per-step VRAM usage.
165	        self.grad_accum_steps = trainer_cfg.get("accumulate_grad_batches", 1)
166	        print(f"  Gradient accumulation steps: {self.grad_accum_steps}")
167	
168	        # torch.compile: fuses CUDA kernels for the encoder (biggest speedup
169	        # on the TCN stack of gated convolutions). Compile only the encoder
170	        # since DSP must stay in float32 for numerical stability.
171	        if trainer_cfg.get("torch_compile", False) and self.device.type == "cuda":
172	            print("  Compiling encoder with torch.compile()...")
173	            self.model.encoder = torch.compile(
174	                self.model.encoder,
175	                mode="reduce-overhead",
176	            )
177	            print("  Compilation complete.")
178	
179	        # Resume from checkpoint if provided
180	        if self.resume_from is not None:
181	            self._load_checkpoint(self.resume_from)
182	
183	    def _build_stage_dataset(self, stage_cfg):
184	        """Build a dataset for a specific curriculum stage."""
185	        data_cfg = self.data_cfg
186	
187	        # Determine which filter types to include
188	        filter_types = stage_cfg.get("filter_types", data_cfg["filter_types"])
189	        # Build type_weights: uniform over active types, 0 for inactive
190	        active_indices = [FILTER_TYPE_TO_IDX[ft] for ft in filter_types]
191	        n_active = len(active_indices)
192	        type_weights = [0.0] * len(FILTER_NAMES)
193	        for idx in active_indices:
194	            type_weights[idx] = 1.0 / n_active
195	
196	        # Get param ranges for this stage (or fall back to data config)
197	        param_ranges = stage_cfg.get("param_ranges", {})
198	        gain_lo, gain_hi = param_ranges.get("gain", data_cfg["gain_bounds"])
199	        # q is a list of [lo, hi]
200	        q_range = param_ranges.get("q", data_cfg["q_bounds"])
201	
202	        # Read duration and size from config instead of hardcoding
203	        duration = data_cfg.get("audio_duration", 1.5)
204	        size = data_cfg.get("dataset_size", 50000)
205	
206	        # Minimum gain threshold per stage: ensures EQ effects are clearly
207	        # visible in the spectrogram during early curriculum stages.
208	        min_gain_db = stage_cfg.get("min_gain_db", 0.0)
209	
210	        dataset = SyntheticEQDataset(
211	            num_bands=self.num_bands,
212	            sample_rate=self.sample_rate,
213	            duration=duration,
214	            n_fft=self.n_fft,
215	            size=size,
216	            gain_range=(gain_lo, gain_hi),
217	            freq_range=tuple(data_cfg["freq_bounds"]),
218	            q_range=tuple(q_range),
219	            type_weights=type_weights,
220	            precompute_mels=False,
221	            n_mels=data_cfg.get("n_mels", 128),
222	            min_gain_db=min_gain_db,
223	        )
224	        dataset.precompute()
225	        return dataset
226	
227	    def _setup_stage(self, stage_cfg):
228	        """Prepare dataset and update loss weights for a curriculum stage."""
229	        print(f"\n{'=' * 60}")
230	        print(f"  Stage: {stage_cfg['name']}")
231	        print(f"{'=' * 60}")
232	
233	        # CRITICAL FIX: Always rebuild dataset for each curriculum stage
234	        # with stage-specific param ranges. Free old loaders first.
235	        if self.train_loader is not None:
236	            print("  Freeing previous stage data loaders...")
237	            del self.train_loader
238	            del self.val_loader
239	            del self.test_loader
240	            del self.test_set
241	            # Explicitly clear the dataset cache list before gc
242	            if hasattr(self, "_stage_dataset") and self._stage_dataset is not None:
243	                if hasattr(self._stage_dataset, "_npz_cache"):
244	                    del self._stage_dataset._npz_cache
245	                del self._stage_dataset
246	            gc.collect()
247	            if torch.cuda.is_available():
248	                torch.cuda.empty_cache()
249	
250	        dataset = self._build_stage_dataset(stage_cfg)
251	        self._stage_dataset = dataset
252	
253	        # Print confirmation of actual param ranges from this stage's dataset
254	        param_ranges = stage_cfg.get("param_ranges", {})
255	        if "gain" in param_ranges:
256	            print(f"  Stage gain range: {param_ranges['gain']}")
257	        if "q" in param_ranges:
258	            print(f"  Stage Q range: {param_ranges['q']}")
259	        print(f"  Dataset size: {len(dataset)} samples")
260	
261	        n_train = int(len(dataset) * self.data_cfg["train_split"])
262	        n_val = int(len(dataset) * self.data_cfg["val_split"])
263	        n_test = len(dataset) - n_train - n_val
264	
265	        # Seeded generator for reproducible splits
266	        gen = torch.Generator().manual_seed(self.cfg.get("seed", 42))
267	        train_set, val_set, test_set = torch.utils.data.random_split(
268	            dataset, [n_train, n_val, n_test], generator=gen
269	        )
270	
271	        num_workers = self.data_cfg.get("num_workers", 4)
272	        pin_memory = self.device.type == "cuda"
273	
274	        self.train_loader = DataLoader(
275	            train_set,
276	            batch_size=self.data_cfg["batch_size"],
277	            shuffle=True,
278	            num_workers=num_workers,
279	            collate_fn=collate_fn,
280	            pin_memory=pin_memory,
281	            drop_last=True,
282	            prefetch_factor=2 if num_workers > 0 else None,
283	            persistent_workers=num_workers > 0,
284	        )
285	        self.val_loader = DataLoader(
286	            val_set,
287	            batch_size=self.data_cfg["batch_size"],
288	            shuffle=False,
289	            num_workers=num_workers,
290	            collate_fn=collate_fn,
291	            pin_memory=pin_memory,
292	            prefetch_factor=2 if num_workers > 0 else None,
293	            persistent_workers=num_workers > 0,
294	        )
295	        self.test_loader = DataLoader(
296	            test_set,
297	            batch_size=self.data_cfg["batch_size"],
298	            shuffle=False,
299	            num_workers=num_workers,
300	            collate_fn=collate_fn,
301	            pin_memory=pin_memory,
302	            prefetch_factor=2 if num_workers > 0 else None,
303	            persistent_workers=num_workers > 0,
304	        )
305	        self.test_set = test_set
306	
307	        # Update loss weights from stage config
308	        if "lambda_type" in stage_cfg:
309	            self.criterion.lambda_type = stage_cfg["lambda_type"]
310	            print(f"  lambda_type = {stage_cfg['lambda_type']}")
311	        if "lambda_spread" in stage_cfg:
312	            self.criterion.lambda_spread = stage_cfg["lambda_spread"]
313	            print(f"  lambda_spread = {stage_cfg['lambda_spread']}")
314	        if "lambda_coverage" in stage_cfg:
315	            self.criterion.lambda_coverage = stage_cfg["lambda_coverage"]
316	            print(f"  lambda_coverage = {stage_cfg['lambda_coverage']}")
317	        if "lambda_freq_anchor" in stage_cfg:
318	            self.criterion.lambda_freq_anchor = stage_cfg["lambda_freq_anchor"]
319	            print(f"  lambda_freq_anchor = {stage_cfg['lambda_freq_anchor']}")
320	        if "lambda_freq_match" in stage_cfg:
321	            self.criterion.param_loss.matcher.lambda_freq = stage_cfg[
322	                "lambda_freq_match"
323	            ]
324	            self.val_matcher.lambda_freq = stage_cfg["lambda_freq_match"]
325	            print(f"  lambda_freq_match = {stage_cfg['lambda_freq_match']}")
326	        if "lambda_q_match" in stage_cfg:
327	            self.criterion.param_loss.matcher.lambda_q = stage_cfg["lambda_q_match"]
328	            self.val_matcher.lambda_q = stage_cfg["lambda_q_match"]
329	            print(f"  lambda_q_match = {stage_cfg['lambda_q_match']}")
330	
331	        # Update Gumbel temperature if model supports it
332	        gumbel_temp = stage_cfg.get("gumbel_temperature", None)
333	        if gumbel_temp is not None and hasattr(self.model, "dsp_cascade"):
334	            if hasattr(self.model.dsp_cascade, "gumbel_temperature"):
335	                self.model.dsp_cascade.gumbel_temperature.fill_(gumbel_temp)
336	                print(f"  gumbel_temperature = {gumbel_temp}")
337	
338	        # Reset LR to base_lr at every stage start so CosineAnnealingLR
339	        # always begins from the full LR, not the decayed value from the
340	        # previous stage. Stages with learning_rate_scale get a fraction.
341	        lr_scale = stage_cfg.get("learning_rate_scale", 1.0)
342	        base_lr = self.cfg["model"]["learning_rate"] * lr_scale
343	        for pg in self.optimizer.param_groups:
344	            pg["lr"] = base_lr
345	        print(f"  lr = {base_lr:.2e}")
346	
347	        # Reset scheduler for this stage with proper eta_min
348	        stage_epochs = stage_cfg["epochs"]
349	        self.scheduler = torch.optim.lr_scheduler.CosineAnnealingLR(
350	            self.optimizer, T_max=stage_epochs, eta_min=base_lr * 0.01
351	        )
352	
353	    def _prepare_input(self, batch):
354	        """Compute mel-spectrogram on GPU from raw audio."""
355	        wet_audio = batch["wet_audio"].to(self.device, non_blocking=True)
356	        mel = self.frontend.mel_spectrogram(wet_audio).squeeze(1)
357	        return mel
358	
359	    def _prepare_dry_mel(self, batch):
360	        """Compute dry mel-spectrogram on GPU from raw audio."""
361	        dry_audio = batch["dry_audio"].to(self.device, non_blocking=True)
362	        return self.frontend.mel_spectrogram(dry_audio).squeeze(1)
363	
364	    def train_one_epoch(self, epoch):
365	        self.model.train()
366	        epoch_loss = 0.0
367	        n_batches = 0
368	        nan_batches = 0
369	
370	        for batch_idx, batch in enumerate(self.train_loader):
371	            mel_frames = self._prepare_input(batch)
372	            target_gain = batch["gain"].to(self.device)
373	            target_freq = batch["freq"].to(self.device)
374	            target_q = batch["q"].to(self.device)
375	            target_ft = batch["filter_type"].to(self.device)
376	
377	            self.optimizer.zero_grad(set_to_none=True)
378	
379	            # Mixed precision training with AMP
380	            if self.use_amp:
381	                dry_mel = self._prepare_dry_mel(batch)
382	                mel_diff = (mel_frames - dry_mel).mean(dim=-1)
383	                mel_profile = mel_diff
384	
385	                with torch.amp.autocast("cuda"):
386	                    embedding, skip_sum = self.model.encoder(mel_frames)
387	
388	                # Parameter head + DSP in full FP32
389	                with torch.amp.autocast("cuda", enabled=False):
390	                    output = self.model._predict_from_embedding(
391	                        embedding, mel_profile=mel_profile
392	                    )
393	
394	                    target_H_mag = self.model.dsp_cascade(
395	                        target_gain.float(),
396	                        target_freq.float(),
397	                        target_q.float(),
398	                        n_fft=self.n_fft,
399	                        filter_type=target_ft,
400	                    ).clamp(min=1e-4, max=1e4)
401	
402	                    total_loss, components = self.criterion(
403	                        output["params"][0],
404	                        output["params"][1],
405	                        output["params"][2],
406	                        output["type_logits"].float(),
407	                        output["H_mag"].float(),
408	                        target_gain,
409	                        target_freq,
410	                        target_q,
411	                        target_ft,
412	                        target_H_mag,
413	                        freq_attn=output.get("freq_attn"),
414	                    )
415	
416	                # Skip batch if loss is NaN/Inf to prevent divergence
417	                if not torch.isfinite(total_loss):
418	                    nan_batches += 1
419	                    self.global_step += 1
420	                    # Reset GradScaler if it's accumulating bad scale
421	                    if nan_batches > 3:
422	                        self.scaler.update()
423	                        nan_batches = 0
424	                    if self.global_step % self.log_every == 0:
425	                        print(
426	                            f"  [train] step={self.global_step} SKIPPING NaN/Inf loss "
427	                            f"(nan_count={nan_batches})"
428	                        )
429	                    continue
430	
431	                # Scale, backward, clip, update with scaler
432	                self.scaler.scale(total_loss).backward()
433	                self.scaler.unscale_(self.optimizer)
434	                # Clip gradients — use value clipping too for FP16 safety
435	                torch.nn.utils.clip_grad_norm_(self.model.parameters(), max_norm=1.0)
436	                # Check for NaN in gradients before stepping
437	                has_nan_grad = any(
438	                    p.grad is not None and not torch.isfinite(p.grad).all()
439	                    for p in self.model.parameters()
440	                )
441	                if has_nan_grad:
442	                    self.scaler.update()
443	                    self.global_step += 1
444	                    nan_batches += 1
445	                    continue
446	                self.scaler.step(self.optimizer)
447	                self.scaler.update()
448	            else:
449	                dry_mel = self._prepare_dry_mel(batch)
450	                mel_diff = (mel_frames - dry_mel).mean(dim=-1)
451	                mel_profile = mel_diff
452	
453	                embedding, skip_sum = self.model.encoder(mel_frames)
454	                output = self.model._predict_from_embedding(
455	                    embedding, mel_profile=mel_profile
456	                )
457	                pred_gain, pred_freq, pred_q = output["params"]
458	
459	                target_H_mag = self.model.dsp_cascade(
460	                    target_gain,
461	                    target_freq,
462	                    target_q,
463	                    n_fft=self.n_fft,
464	                    filter_type=target_ft,
465	                ).clamp(min=1e-4, max=1e4)
466	
467	                total_loss, components = self.criterion(
468	                    pred_gain,
469	                    pred_freq,
470	                    pred_q,
471	                    output["type_logits"],
472	                    output["H_mag"],
473	                    target_gain,
474	                    target_freq,
475	                    target_q,
476	                    target_ft,
477	                    target_H_mag,
478	                    freq_attn=output.get("freq_attn"),
479	                )
480	                if not torch.isfinite(total_loss):
481	                    nan_batches += 1
482	                    self.global_step += 1
483	                    if self.global_step % self.log_every == 0:
484	                        print(
485	                            f"  [train] step={self.global_step} SKIPPING NaN/Inf loss"
486	                        )
487	                    continue
488	
489	                total_loss.backward()
490	                torch.nn.utils.clip_grad_norm_(self.model.parameters(), max_norm=1.0)
491	                self.optimizer.step()
492	
493	            # Check model parameters for NaN (early warning of corruption)
494	            if self.global_step % 100 == 0:
495	                self._check_model_health()
496	
497	            epoch_loss += total_loss.item()
498	            n_batches += 1
499	            self.global_step += 1
500	
501	            if self.global_step % self.log_every == 0:
502	                lr = self.optimizer.param_groups[0]["lr"]
503	                parts = [
504	                    f"step={self.global_step} loss={total_loss.item():.4f} lr={lr:.2e}"
505	                ]
506	                for k, v in components.items():
507	                    parts.append(f"{k}={v:.4f}")
508	                print("  [train] " + " | ".join(parts))
509	
510	        if nan_batches > 0:
511	            print(
512	                f"  [train] epoch={epoch}: {nan_batches} NaN batches skipped "
513	                f"out of {n_batches + nan_batches} total"
514	            )
515	        return epoch_loss / max(n_batches, 1)
516	
517	    def _check_model_health(self):
518	        """Check model parameters for NaN/Inf and warn if detected."""
519	        for name, param in self.model.named_parameters():
520	            if not torch.isfinite(param).all():
521	                print(
522	                    f"  WARNING: NaN/Inf in model parameter '{name}' "
523	                    f"(shape={param.shape})"
524	                )
525	
526	    @torch.no_grad()
527	    def validate(self, epoch, use_test_loader=False):
528	        self.model.eval()
529	        val_loss = 0.0
530	        n_batches = 0
531	        param_maes = {
532	            "gain": [],
533	            "freq": [],
534	            "q": [],
535	            "type_acc": [],
536	            "freq_span": [],
537	            "freq_err_low": [],
538	            "freq_err_mid": [],
539	            "freq_err_high": [],
540	        }
541	
542	        loader = (
543	            self.test_loader
544	            if use_test_loader and self.test_loader is not None
545	            else self.val_loader
546	        )
547	
548	        # Use autocast for validation too when AMP is enabled
549	        autocast_ctx = (
550	            torch.amp.autocast("cuda", enabled=self.use_amp)
551	            if self.use_amp
552	            else torch.no_grad()
553	        )
554	
555	        with autocast_ctx:
556	            for batch in loader:
557	                mel_frames = self._prepare_input(batch)
558	                target_gain = batch["gain"].to(self.device)
559	                target_freq = batch["freq"].to(self.device)
560	                target_q = batch["q"].to(self.device)
561	                target_ft = batch["filter_type"].to(self.device)
562	
563	                dry_mel = self._prepare_dry_mel(batch)
564	                mel_diff = (mel_frames - dry_mel).mean(dim=-1)
565	                mel_profile = mel_diff
566	
567	                # Manual forward to pass mel_diff as mel_profile
568	                if self.use_amp:
569	                    with torch.amp.autocast("cuda"):
570	                        embedding, skip_sum = self.model.encoder(mel_frames)
571	                    with torch.amp.autocast("cuda", enabled=False):
572	                        output = self.model._predict_from_embedding(
573	                            embedding, mel_profile=mel_profile
574	                        )
575	                else:
576	                    embedding, skip_sum = self.model.encoder(mel_frames)
577	                    output = self.model._predict_from_embedding(
578	                        embedding, mel_profile=mel_profile
579	                    )
580	                pred_gain, pred_freq, pred_q = output["params"]
581	
582	                target_H_mag = self.model.dsp_cascade(
583	                    target_gain,
584	                    target_freq,
585	                    target_q,
586	                    n_fft=self.n_fft,
587	                    filter_type=target_ft,
588	                )
589	
590	                total_loss, _ = self.criterion(
591	                    pred_gain,
592	                    pred_freq,
593	                    pred_q,
594	                    output["type_logits"],
595	                    output["H_mag"],
596	                    target_gain,
597	                    target_freq,
598	                    target_q,
599	                    target_ft,
600	                    target_H_mag,
601	                    freq_attn=output.get("freq_attn"),
602	                )
603	
604	                val_loss += total_loss.item()
605	                n_batches += 1
606	
607	                # Use Hungarian matching for accurate permutation-invariant metrics
608	                matched = self.val_matcher(
609	                    pred_gain.cpu(),
610	                    pred_freq.cpu(),
611	                    pred_q.cpu(),
612	                    target_gain.cpu(),
613	                    target_freq.cpu(),
614	                    target_q.cpu(),
615	                    output["type_logits"].cpu(),
616	                    target_ft.cpu(),
617	                )
618	                if len(matched) == 4:
619	                    m_gain, m_freq, m_q, m_ft = matched
620	                else:
621	                    m_gain, m_freq, m_q = matched
622	                    m_ft = target_ft.cpu()
623	
624	                param_maes["gain"].append(
625	                    (pred_gain.cpu() - m_gain).abs().mean().item()
626	                )
627	                param_maes["freq"].append(
628	                    torch.clamp(
629	                        (torch.log2(pred_freq.cpu() / m_freq.clamp(min=1e-3))).abs(),
630	                        max=4.0,
631	                    )
632	                    .mean()
633	                    .item()
634	                )
635	                param_maes["q"].append(
636	                    (torch.log10(pred_q.cpu() / m_q.clamp(min=1e-3)))
637	                    .abs()
638	                    .mean()
639	                    .item()
640	                )
641	                param_maes["type_acc"].append(
642	                    (output["filter_type"].cpu() == m_ft).float().mean().item()
643	                )
644	
645	                # Frequency span: how many octaves the predicted bands cover
646	                sorted_pf, _ = torch.sort(pred_freq.cpu(), dim=-1)
647	                pred_span = torch.log2(
648	                    sorted_pf[:, -1] / sorted_pf[:, 0].clamp(min=1e-5)
649	                )
650	                param_maes["freq_span"].append(pred_span.mean().item())
651	
652	                # Per-region frequency error breakdown
653	                log_err = (torch.log2(pred_freq.cpu() / m_freq.clamp(min=1e-5))).abs()
654	                log_mf = torch.log2(m_freq.clamp(min=1e-5))
655	
656	                low_mask = log_mf < math.log2(500)
657	                mid_mask = (log_mf >= math.log2(500)) & (log_mf < math.log2(4000))
658	                high_mask = log_mf >= math.log2(4000)
659	
660	                if low_mask.any():
661	                    param_maes["freq_err_low"].append(log_err[low_mask].mean().item())
662	                if mid_mask.any():
663	                    param_maes["freq_err_mid"].append(log_err[mid_mask].mean().item())
664	                if high_mask.any():
665	                    param_maes["freq_err_high"].append(log_err[high_mask].mean().item())
666	
667	        avg_val_loss = val_loss / max(n_batches, 1)
668	        metrics = {k: sum(v) / len(v) for k, v in param_maes.items() if v}
669	
670	        print(
671	            f"  [val] epoch={epoch} val_loss={avg_val_loss:.4f} "
672	            f"gain_mae={metrics.get('gain', 0):.2f}dB "
673	            f"freq_mae={metrics.get('freq', 0):.3f}oct "
674	            f"q_mae={metrics.get('q', 0):.3f}dec "
675	            f"type_acc={metrics.get('type_acc', 0):.1%} "
676	            f"freq_span={metrics.get('freq_span', 0):.1f}oct "
677	            f"freq_err=[{metrics.get('freq_err_low', 0):.2f} "
678	            f"{metrics.get('freq_err_mid', 0):.2f} "
679	            f"{metrics.get('freq_err_high', 0):.2f}]"
680	        )
681	        return avg_val_loss, metrics
682	
683	    def save_checkpoint(self, epoch, val_loss, is_best=False, stage_name=""):
684	        state = {
685	            "epoch": epoch,
686	            "stage": stage_name,
687	            "model_state_dict": self.model.state_dict(),
688	            "optimizer_state_dict": self.optimizer.state_dict(),
689	            "scheduler_state_dict": self.scheduler.state_dict(),
690	            "val_loss": val_loss,
691	            "best_val_loss": self.best_val_loss,
692	            "global_step": self.global_step,
693	            "history": self.history,
694	        }
695	        path = self.ckpt_dir / f"epoch_{epoch:03d}.pt"
696	        torch.save(state, path)
697	        if is_best:
698	            best_path = self.ckpt_dir / "best.pt"
699	            torch.save(state, best_path)
700	        print(f"  Saved checkpoint: {path}" + (" (best)" if is_best else ""))
701	
702	        # Save history incrementally after every epoch
703	        hist_path = self.ckpt_dir / "training_history.json"
704	        with open(hist_path, "w") as f:
705	            json.dump(self.history, f, indent=2, default=str)
706	
707	        # Checkpoint cleanup: keep best.pt + last 3 epoch checkpoints
708	        # Never delete best.pt
709	        epoch_ckpts = sorted(self.ckpt_dir.glob("epoch_*.pt"))
710	        for old_ckpt in epoch_ckpts[:-3]:  # Keep only last 3
711	            old_ckpt.unlink()
712	            print(f"  Cleaned up old checkpoint: {old_ckpt}")
713	
714	    def _load_checkpoint(self, checkpoint_path):
715	        """Load checkpoint and restore training state."""
716	        print(f"  Loading checkpoint from {checkpoint_path}")
717	        checkpoint = torch.load(
718	            checkpoint_path, map_location=self.device, weights_only=True
719	        )
720	
721	        self.model.load_state_dict(checkpoint["model_state_dict"])
722	        self.optimizer.load_state_dict(checkpoint["optimizer_state_dict"])
723	
724	        # Restore scheduler state if available (may fail if scheduler config changed)
725	        if "scheduler_state_dict" in checkpoint and hasattr(self, "scheduler"):
726	            try:
727	                self.scheduler.load_state_dict(checkpoint["scheduler_state_dict"])
728	            except Exception as e:
729	                print(f"  Warning: Could not restore scheduler state: {e}")
730	
731	        # Restore training state
732	        self.global_step = checkpoint.get("global_step", 0)
733	        self.best_val_loss = checkpoint.get("best_val_loss", float("inf"))
734	
735	        # Load training history if available
736	        import json
737	
738	        hist_path = self.ckpt_dir / "training_history.json"
739	        if hist_path.exists():
740	            try:
741	                with open(hist_path, "r") as f:
742	                    self.history = json.load(f)
743	            except Exception as e:
744	                print(f"  Warning: Could not load training history: {e}")
745	
746	        # FIX: Restore history from checkpoint so resumed runs append correctly
747	        if "history" in checkpoint:
748	            self.history = checkpoint["history"]
749	            print(f"  Restored {len(self.history)} history entries from checkpoint")
750	        else:
751	            # Fallback: try loading from training_history.json on disk
752	            hist_path = self.ckpt_dir / "training_history.json"
753	            if hist_path.exists():
754	                with open(hist_path, "r") as f:
755	                    self.history = json.load(f)
756	                print(f"  Restored {len(self.history)} history entries from disk")
757	            else:
758	                print("  WARNING: No training history found — starting fresh")
759	
760	        # Calculate epoch offset for resuming
761	        start_epoch = checkpoint.get("epoch", 0)
762	        self.start_epoch = start_epoch
763	
764	        print(f"  Resumed from epoch {start_epoch}, global_step={self.global_step}")
765	        print(f"  Best val loss: {self.best_val_loss:.4f}")
766	
767	    def fit(self):
768	        total_params = sum(p.numel() for p in self.model.parameters())
769	        print("IDSP Multi-Type EQ Estimator Training")
770	        print(f"  Device: {self.device}")
771	        print(f"  Parameters: {total_params:,}")
772	        print(f"  Max epochs: {self.max_epochs}")
773	        print(f"  Receptive field: {self.model.receptive_field_frames} frames")
774	
775	        if self.stages:
776	            print(f"  Curriculum: {len(self.stages)} stages")
777	            for s in self.stages:
778	                print(f"    - {s['name']}: {s['epochs']} epochs")
779	
780	        # Get starting epoch from resume or default to 0
781	        start_epoch = getattr(self, "start_epoch", 0)
782	        global_epoch = 0
783	
784	        if self.stages:
785	            # Curriculum-based training
786	            for stage in self.stages:
787	                # FIX: Skip entire stages that were already completed before resuming
788	                stage_start = global_epoch
789	                stage_end = global_epoch + stage["epochs"]
790	
791	                if stage_end <= start_epoch:
792	                    # This stage was fully completed — skip it entirely
793	                    print(
794	                        f"  Skipping fully completed stage: {stage['name']} "
795	                        f"(epochs {stage_start + 1}-{stage_end})"
796	                    )
797	                    global_epoch = stage_end
798	                    continue
799	
800	                self._setup_stage(stage)
801	
802	                for local_epoch in range(1, stage["epochs"] + 1):
803	                    global_epoch += 1
804	
805	                    # Skip epochs that were already completed if resuming
806	                    if global_epoch <= start_epoch:
807	                        print(
808	                            f"  Skipping epoch {global_epoch}/{self.max_epochs} (already completed)"
809	                        )
810	                        # Still need to step scheduler to maintain state
811	                        self.scheduler.step()
812	                        continue
813	
814	                    t0 = time.time()
815	                    train_loss = self.train_one_epoch(global_epoch)
816	                    val_loss, metrics = self.validate(global_epoch)
817	                    self.scheduler.step()
818	                    elapsed = time.time() - t0
819	
820	                    is_best = val_loss < self.best_val_loss
821	                    if is_best:
822	                        self.best_val_loss = val_loss
823	                    self.save_checkpoint(global_epoch, val_loss, is_best, stage["name"])
824	
825	                    self.history.append(
826	                        {
827	                            "epoch": global_epoch,
828	                            "stage": stage["name"],
829	                            "train_loss": train_loss,
830	                            "val_loss": val_loss,
831	                            "metrics": metrics,
832	                            "time_s": elapsed,
833	                        }
834	                    )
835	
836	                    hist_path = self.ckpt_dir / "training_history.json"
837	                    with open(hist_path, "w") as f:
838	                        json.dump(self.history, f, indent=2, default=str)
839	
840	                    print(
841	                        f"Epoch {global_epoch}/{self.max_epochs} [{stage['name']}] "
842	                        f"({elapsed:.1f}s) train={train_loss:.4f} val={val_loss:.4f}"
843	                    )
844	        else:
845	            # Flat training (no curriculum)
846	            self._setup_flat_training()
847	            self.scheduler = torch.optim.lr_scheduler.CosineAnnealingLR(
848	                self.optimizer, T_max=self.max_epochs, eta_min=1e-6
849	            )
850	
851	            start_epoch = getattr(self, "start_epoch", 0)
852	            for epoch in range(start_epoch + 1, self.max_epochs + 1):
853	                t0 = time.time()
854	                train_loss = self.train_one_epoch(epoch)
855	                val_loss, metrics = self.validate(epoch)
856	                self.scheduler.step()
857	                elapsed = time.time() - t0
858	
859	                is_best = val_loss < self.best_val_loss
860	                if is_best:
861	                    self.best_val_loss = val_loss
862	                self.save_checkpoint(epoch, val_loss, is_best)
863	
864	                self.history.append(
865	                    {
866	                        "epoch": epoch,
867	                        "train_loss": train_loss,
868	                        "val_loss": val_loss,
869	                        "metrics": metrics,
870	                        "time_s": elapsed,
871	                    }
872	                )
873	
874	                hist_path = self.ckpt_dir / "training_history.json"
875	                with open(hist_path, "w") as f:
876	                    json.dump(self.history, f, indent=2, default=str)
877	
878	                print(
879	                    f"Epoch {epoch}/{self.max_epochs} ({elapsed:.1f}s) "
880	                    f"train={train_loss:.4f} val={val_loss:.4f}"
881	                )
882	
883	        hist_path = self.ckpt_dir / "training_history.json"
884	        with open(hist_path, "w") as f:
885	            json.dump(self.history, f, indent=2, default=str)
886	        print(f"\nTraining complete. Best val loss: {self.best_val_loss:.4f}")
887	        print(f"History saved to {hist_path}")
888	
889	        # Test evaluation at end of training
890	        if self.test_loader is not None:
891	            print(f"\n{'=' * 60}")
892	            print("  Test Evaluation")
893	            print(f"{'=' * 60}")
894	            test_loss, test_metrics = self.validate(
895	                epoch=self.max_epochs, use_test_loader=True
896	            )
897	            print(f"  Test loss: {test_loss:.4f}")
898	            print("  Test metrics:")
899	            for k, v in test_metrics.items():
900	                print(f"    {k}: {v:.4f}")
901	            print(f"  Best val loss: {self.best_val_loss:.4f}")
902	            print(f"{'=' * 60}\n")
903	
904	    def _setup_flat_training(self):
905	        """Setup single-stage training (no curriculum)."""
906	        data_cfg = self.data_cfg
907	
908	        # Read duration and size from config instead of hardcoding
909	        duration = data_cfg.get("audio_duration", 1.5)
910	        size = data_cfg.get("dataset_size", 50000)
911	
912	        # Build type_weights from filter_types list (handles peaking-only correctly)
913	        filter_types_cfg = data_cfg.get(
914	            "filter_types", ["peaking", "lowshelf", "highshelf", "highpass", "lowpass"]
915	        )
916	        active_indices = [FILTER_TYPE_TO_IDX[ft] for ft in filter_types_cfg]
917	        n_active = len(active_indices)
918	        type_weights = [0.0] * len(FILTER_NAMES)
919	        for idx in active_indices:
920	            type_weights[idx] = 1.0 / n_active
921	
922	        dataset = SyntheticEQDataset(
923	            num_bands=self.num_bands,
924	            sample_rate=self.sample_rate,
925	            duration=duration,
926	            n_fft=self.n_fft,
927	            size=size,
928	            gain_range=tuple(data_cfg["gain_bounds"]),
929	            freq_range=tuple(data_cfg["freq_bounds"]),
930	            q_range=tuple(data_cfg["q_bounds"]),
931	            type_weights=type_weights,
932	            precompute_mels=False,
933	            n_mels=data_cfg.get("n_mels", 128),
934	        )
935	        dataset.precompute()
936	
937	        n_train = int(len(dataset) * data_cfg["train_split"])
938	        n_val = int(len(dataset) * data_cfg["val_split"])
939	        n_test = len(dataset) - n_train - n_val
940	
941	        # Seeded generator for reproducible splits
942	        gen = torch.Generator().manual_seed(self.cfg.get("seed", 42))
943	        train_set, val_set, test_set = torch.utils.data.random_split(
944	            dataset, [n_train, n_val, n_test], generator=gen
945	        )
946	
947	        num_workers = data_cfg.get("num_workers", 4)
948	        pin_memory = self.device.type == "cuda"
949	
950	        self.train_loader = DataLoader(
951	            train_set,
952	            batch_size=data_cfg["batch_size"],
953	            shuffle=True,
954	            num_workers=num_workers,
955	            collate_fn=collate_fn,
956	            pin_memory=pin_memory,
957	            drop_last=True,
958	            prefetch_factor=2 if num_workers > 0 else None,
959	            persistent_workers=num_workers > 0,
960	        )
961	        self.val_loader = DataLoader(
962	            val_set,
963	            batch_size=data_cfg["batch_size"],
964	            shuffle=False,
965	            num_workers=num_workers,
966	            collate_fn=collate_fn,
967	            pin_memory=pin_memory,
968	            prefetch_factor=2 if num_workers > 0 else None,
969	            persistent_workers=num_workers > 0,
970	        )
971	        self.test_loader = DataLoader(
972	            test_set,
973	            batch_size=data_cfg["batch_size"],
974	            shuffle=False,
975	            num_workers=num_workers,
976	            collate_fn=collate_fn,
977	            pin_memory=pin_memory,
978	            prefetch_factor=2 if num_workers > 0 else None,
979	            persistent_workers=num_workers > 0,
980	        )
981	        self.test_set = test_set
982	
983	
984	if __name__ == "__main__":
985	    import argparse
986	
987	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser()
988	    parser.add_argument("--config", type=str, default="conf/config.yaml")
989	    parser.add_argument("--resume", type=str, default=None)
990	    args = parser.parse_args()
991	
992	    trainer = Trainer(config_path=args.config, resume_from=args.resume)
993	    trainer.fit()
994
